narr.Aqua Shard: Aqua Shard's traffic gap is the story: 24,839 users in W23, about 22% below the same FY week last year, and conversion rate has now slipped too (4.68% vs 5.70%). GA4 bookings of 1,163 are well short of last July's 1,807. Google spend was pushed hard in W23 (£2,887, up from £1,859) and CPA rose to £4.93 — still efficient, but the extra budget is buying volume at a higher price. Meta held at £13.67 CPA on 142 schedules. Walk-in signals dropped sharply to 658.
narr.Hutong: Hutong is the only venue with users ahead of last year (14,250, +4.6%), but the funnel is the problem: conversion rate fell to 4.70% against 6.00% a year ago, so GA4 bookings landed at 670 vs 817 LY. Google spend was cut to £1,206 in W23 (142 conversions, £8.50 CPA — still well above last year's £2.59). Meta improved to £12.37 on a record 137 schedules. Walk-in signals fell to 206 as local-ad delivery eased.
narr.Regent St: Regent St (Kyoto + Nueva combined) keeps converting better than last year — 4.30% vs 3.87% — but the traffic base has thinned to 7,890 users, ~22% below LY, which drags GA4 bookings to 339 vs 393. Google spend doubled WoW to £1,329 for 68 conversions (£19.55 CPA, the worst in the set). Meta sits at £17.23 on 71 schedules, still far better than last year's £30.91. The lever here is top-of-funnel volume, not conversion.
narr.Azzurra: Azzurra's users fell to 2,001, 15.2% below LY, and conversion softened slightly to 4.95% (5.12% LY), leaving GA4 bookings at 99 vs 121. Google spend was pulled back to £349 and CPA improved to £12.92 on 27 conversions. Meta scaled up to £1,139 spend for 60 schedules at £18.98 CPA — a huge improvement on last year's £93. Walk-in signals collapsed to 27 from 106 the prior week as local ads were throttled.
narr.Shiro Sushi: Shiro Sushi is volume-capped but converting exceptionally: 1,321 users (down 45% YoY) yet a 9.39% conversion rate against 4.66% last year, so GA4 bookings actually rose to 124 vs 112 LY. Google delivered 60 bookings at £11.08 CPA on £665 spend, and Meta ran at £11.19 — the cheapest in the portfolio. Walk-in signals recovered to 314. Fixing the traffic shortfall is the single biggest opportunity in the set.
narr.DSL: DSL (Dim Sum Library) leads the portfolio on conversion at 9.39% on 6,121 users, with GA4 bookings of 575. NOTE: DSL rebranded, so the 2025 lines are pre-rebrand (Luci) and are NOT a like-for-like comparison — the apparent YoY gains are a rebrand artefact, not a real uplift, and last year's Google data does not exist on this account. Google spend was cut to £371 in W23 (27 bookings, £13.75 CPA) while Meta ran its heaviest week at £1,855 for 97 schedules (£19.12). No Google local-action conversions are configured, so walk-in signals show 0.

Aqua Shard: Latest-week users down 21.6% vs same FY week last year; conversion rate also down
Hutong: Users up 4.6% YoY but conversion rate down to 4.70% from 6.00%
Regent St: Latest-week users down 22.4% YoY despite conversion rate ahead of last year
Azzurra: Latest-week users down 15.2% YoY and conversion rate slightly softer
Shiro Sushi: Latest-week users down 45.1% YoY — volume-capped, though conversion is double last year
DSL: Pre-rebrand (Luci) baseline last year — YoY lines are not a like-for-like comparison

sharednote.Regent St: Regent St combines Aqua Kyoto + Aqua Nueva (GA4 users/bookings & Meta spend/schedules summed; Google is a single shared account — Kyoto + Nueva booking actions summed). Walk-in signals are the shared Google account's local actions.
sharednote.Hutong: Google bookings sum two booking actions ('Sevenrooms Booking Complete' + 'Hutong UK - GA4 (web) sevenrooms_booking_complete'). No 'Click Book Now' conversion action exists on this account, so walk-in signals cover Store visits + Directions + Menu views + Clicks to call.
sharednote.Shiro Sushi: Google bookings sum three booking actions (SST booking_complete + UK sevenrooms_booking_complete + Booking Complete).
sharednote.Azzurra: Google account has no Store-visit conversion; walk-in signals cover Directions + Menu views + Clicks to call only, and no local actions were recorded last year (LY = 0).
sharednote.DSL: DSL Meta account reports as 'Luci' (confirmed mapping). Google account has no 2025 data (post-rebrand build) and no local-action conversions, so LY Google bookings/CPA and all walk-in signals show 0.
